Transcrição Completa: 76 How to Compliantly say Uncompliant Things
INTRODUÇÃO E CONTEXTO
[00:00] All right, cool. So, welcome in everybody. Um, to be clear, this week's group call, we had a request from Drew. He asked, well, let me just give you a background. So, first of all, he sent me a message and he was saying, "Hey, why is this ad getting disapproved?" And his ads had all kinds of non-compliance stuff on it.
[00:20] He mentioned the words credit score. He literally put the credit score range that people would ideally be in who respond to his ads on the ad itself. And uh, yeah, I mean, he just he just wasn't aware of the compliance rules.
[00:35] And you know, you have to learn how to finesse. Okay? When you want to successfully get away with saying things that are technically non-compliant, you have to learn the art of being able to say these things without directly saying them.
A ARTE DA CONFORMIDADE
[00:50] Okay? So, to be clear, I have a bunch of these individual lessons I can go through. And I also want to give you guys an opportunity if there's something specific that has essentially impacted you that you feel confused about as to why it got disapproved or there's something specifically that you want to say that you haven't been able to get away with saying, uh, please feel free to put that into the chat.
[01:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Art of Compliant Non-Compliant Ads"
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico neste slide
VISUAL: Título principal destacando o conceito de transformar anúncios não-conformes em conformes, layout profissional de apresentação
[01:20] So, to be clear, and and just to start this off, uh this is the art of compliant non-compliant ads. Okay. So, when I initially started doing this, it was for the uh info niche mainly with people that were like teaching make money strategies.
[01:35] And a lot of these people, they say the dumbest shit. Uh they use all these specific buzzwords that will get you denied 100% of the time. Um sometimes it's wealth, uh sometimes it's passive income. Um, these are just examples to be clear.
[01:50] A lot of the times they'll also put like make, you know, x amount of dollars in, you know, x time frame. This is another like super non-compliant example.
EDUCAÇÃO EM CONFORMIDADE
[02:05] And just to get started with all this, it's important to note like this all starts with the fact that you're aware and educated about what's technically compliant and non-compliant. So, just for starters, put into the put into the chat for me, when is the last time that you took whatever advertising channel you're using. When is the last time you went in there and took their compliance course that they all make publicly available for free?
[02:30] Two years ago, never. What course? Four years ago. Yeah. You see what course? Yeah.
[02:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Meta Blueprint - Compliance Section"
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico
VISUAL: Referência à plataforma Meta Blueprint, seção de conformidade destacada, estrutura de curso educacional
[02:45] So as an example, you know, like this is where it all starts. Like you got to know what is and is not compliant. So every single ad platform, like Facebook has an example after they renamed themselves to Meta, they call it the Meta Blueprint. Um, and they have an entire section dedicated to compliance.
[03:05] Okay? And they openly tell you specifically like with examples like here's every single one of our compliance policies. And some of them are like literal laws and regulations.
EXEMPLO: FAIR CREDIT ACT
[03:20] So, as an example, like Drew just with his ad talking about discriminating on credit scores literally broke a law. Okay, so long story short, uh it's called the Fair it's called the Fair Credit Act.
[03:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Fair Credit Act Violation"
NÚMEROS: 700+ credit score (exemplo de discriminação)
VISUAL: Destaque legal de violação, ícone de aviso, referência a crime financeiro misdemeanor
[03:40] Okay, and moral of the story, it's like an actual set of regulations. It's technically considered a financial crime, you know. So, he'd get hit with a misdemeanor financial crime in the instance that the regulating body wanted to criminally prosecute him, which, you know, is very, very, very low probability. But regardless, I mean, he literally broke a law. He didn't even know, you know.
[04:05] So, they'll it's it's important to note like what like why the fuck does Facebook care about compliance? And it's and it's pretty simple. They do not want to get fined. Okay? Or even worse, criminally prosecuted. Okay?
[04:20] So, Facebook and all these other ad platforms, they're just trying to protect their ass. Okay? That's it.
CONFORMIDADE INTERNACIONAL
[04:30] So, when you're unaware, and and this goes for all the countries, by the way, like the EU in general, as an example, for all those countries that make up that coalition, they have their own set of individual compliance standards.
[04:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "International Compliance Standards - EU & Germany"
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico
VISUAL: Mapa ou referência geográfica à União Europeia, destaque para Alemanha como tendo padrões mais rigorosos
[04:50] like not just the EU in general but like you know Germany has their own set of individual compliance standards some of the most strict on earth you know so here's my point okay we have to be aware of wherever we're targeting what the specific laws regulations and on the platform what specific you know things they're going to protect against and what they're not okay.
A ARTE DA FINESSE - EXEMPLO 1
[05:15] so when I say there's an art of turning non-compliant ads into compliant ads Just let me give you a direct example. Okay. So what's bad to say is passive income. Okay. What is okay to say is passive returns. You see that? Just a little finesse. Okay? Just a little finesse. That's all it takes to make that one compliant. You see that's it.
[05:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Synonym Strategy"
❌ Passive Income
✅ Passive Returns
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico
VISUAL: Comparação lado a lado com X vermelho para não-conforme e check verde para conforme, layout de antes/depois
CONTEXTO É TUDO
[05:50] Now, to be clear, sometimes, and this one's this one's you can sometimes get away with saying wealth to be clear. It just depends if you're a highly legitimate organization or not.
[06:05] Most of the people that say the word wealth are with, you know, they wouldn't admit this themselves, but they're the equivalent of like a literal scammer in the eyes of everybody else on Earth. Even if they're not a scammer, you know.
[06:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Context Matters: The Wealth Example"
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico
VISUAL: Comparação visual entre dois cenários - "Scammer Look" vs "Professional Look"
[06:25] like if if there's a guy who's wearing like Miami attire, you know, like let's say it's like a fucking Balenciaga shirt, uh, some shit designer jeans, and like designer shoes, and he's standing by a car, and he's like, "Yeah, so you want to build wealth?" It's like regardless if that guy's highly legitimate or not, they're going to take that as a non-compliant ad example, okay?
[06:50] Versus, let's use the opposite, which is somebody who is wearing like a nice button-down shirt. They're in like an office setting and they literally have uh multiple financial uh certificates, you know, they are literally licensed to talk about money and helping people manage their money.
[07:10] Okay, that person specifically saying something about creating long-term wealth through some type of investment vehicle that they might be plugging is completely different. Okay.
[07:25] So, it's also important to note the context of how you are communicating things can get you denied or can get you approved. You see, does that one make sense?

[07:40] This is probably one of the biggest ones that I want to make sure you can understand. Okay? [09:10] Okay, you you can deny them, but you can't like openly discriminate. Okay, that's what makes this non-compliant. He is obviously non he is obviously discriminating here, right? Everybody sees that clear as day that there's no ifands or buts about it.
[09:25] Oh, yeah.
[09:30] Qualifications. You have to have a 700 plus credit score. You want to quit your nineto-five job. Okay. And what's the background of this video? Just what's happening in the background here?

[09:50] Yeah, just you walking on a beach. Yeah. Somebody tell somebody tell Drew here what what's actually implied with him walking on the beach.
[10:00] You don't have to work to earn.

Using Non-Compliant Words Compliantly
Speaking Against vs Speaking For
Educational vs Promotional Context

[13:45] And to be clear, this is critical to understand. Okay. So, Tai will take the environment and use it as a context for an implied claim without saying it out loud.
[14:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Amateur vs Professional Approach"
❌ Amateur: Direct Income Claims
✅ Professional: Environmental Implications
NÚMEROS: $10,000-$100,000/month, 60 days (exemplo amador)
VISUAL: Comparação split-screen, lado amador com texto direto e números, lado profissional com ambiente luxuoso sem claims diretas
SCRIPT AMADOR VS PROFISSIONAL
[14:05] So, an amateur would say, "Hey, if you want to get a lot richer and you want to be able to buy a bunch of exotic cars and like a really nice house, I'm going to teach you how to start a social media marketing agency. And all I need you to do is just click on this link here. I put together a whole a whole webinar for you going through all the details of how it works, how you can get paid $10,000 to $100,000 a month in as little as 60 days.
[14:35] You know, that's a fucking novice, okay? A non-compliant novice, okay?

VISUAL: Script breakdown mostrando uso de linguagem vaga, foco em oportunidade vs garantias, ambiente fazendo o trabalho de vendas
[14:50] Versus what Tai does. Tai will just stand there in front of an $80,000 a month Beverly Hills rental, okay, with four very respected and well-known exotic cars. And he'll just tell you in a very compliant way, you know, one of the greatest opportunities that exists in this day and age is capitalizing on social media. Okay?
[15:15] Now, notice notice as an example of that capitalizing. Okay. Taking advantage of Okay. One of the greatest opportunities that exists in our day and age, this modern day and age, is capitalizing on social media. Okay. There's more opportunities on these platforms than ever before.
[15:35] Businesses need your help. There are x amount of businesses just in the United States alone that don't regularly post content on their social media. We all know social media can generate revenue for businesses. But the businesses, they don't have the skills to adapt to that. And that's where somebody like you can come in.
[15:55] You can create content on behalf of these businesses. You can work with these businesses to take ads on social media platforms and help them generate customers from those ads. You could help them just build websites and funnels and emails. There's all kinds of stuff that you could sell these businesses.
[16:15] And you know the craziest part, businesses will pay you a lot. I mean, I've I've had guys who go through my programs that are making thousands or tens of thousands of dollars and they get paid that every month from these clients that they're able to retain.

"Thousands or tens of thousands"
"Every month" (separated from amount)
Filler words between claims
NÚMEROS: Thousands to tens of thousands (vago), monthly (separado)
VISUAL: Destaque de técnica de espaçamento, setas mostrando separação entre valores e tempo, palavras de preenchimento destacadas
[16:35] And the businesses, they're happy to pay it. They need the help. This opportunity, I call it the SMMA, social media marketing agency. If you're a young person and you want to take advantage of something like this, I put together a video for you.
[16:50] You can check it out on this next page. I go through the three things that you need to know in order to in order to get involved in this. If you're interested, you know, press that button. Excited to go through this with you. And you know, hopefully it's going to change your life like it has many others.

[17:30] And anyway, long story short, u he's also saying all this in front of a fucking mansion that he paid 80K a month for. Okay, Tai doesn't have to sit there and hard flex on you about with words. Okay, that's the thing. It's like with words, he doesn't have to say these things.
[17:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Environmental Claims vs Verbal Claims"
Cars: Aventador, 458/488, Gallardo, Maserati
Mansion: $80K/month rental
NO verbal flex needed
NÚMEROS: $80,000/month, 4 carros exóticos
VISUAL: Lista de ativos visuais, comparação entre claims verbais (proibidas) vs claims ambientais (permitidas), ícones de carros e mansão
TÉCNICA DE ESPAÇAMENTO
[17:55] And in addition to that, notice notice what else he would do in that ad. He'd say, "Yeah, there's people that, you know, have gone through this program and that do this that make thousands of dollars to tens of thousands of dollars. Hey, and by the way, you know, these businesses are paying everybody monthly, you know, so you can close people and as long as you can retain their business, you know, you're getting set up every month for an opportunity to continue working with these people."
[18:25] Think of what I just said. I made the claim of you can make X in X time frame, but I didn't literally say it like that. You see, I didn't flat up say, "Hey, if you want to get paid $10,000 a month in as little as 60 days.
[18:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Filler Word Technique"
Direct: "$10K/month in 60 days" ❌
Indirect: "Thousands...tens of thousands...monthly...couple months" ✅
NÚMEROS: $10,000/month, 60 days (exemplo errado); thousands to tens of thousands (exemplo correto)
VISUAL: Comparação de frases, palavras de preenchimento destacadas em cores diferentes, setas mostrando fluxo de informação espaçada
[18:45] you say, "Yeah, there's people who go through this program that come out the other side and they make thousands of dollars. Some of them even even make tens of thousands of dollars per deal that they'll do. And businesses, they're happy to pay that every month. Okay?
[19:05] And here's the crazy part, like it doesn't take more than a couple months to get started and all that. You know, you just got to learn what to do and you got to go out there and you got to pitch businesses and you just got to generate some opportunities for yourself.
[19:20] And like I said, these businesses need it. You know, they need your help. You see, can you see how you take something that's non-compliant and make it compliant in that example? It's like the art of finesse. Okay.
REFORMULANDO O ANÚNCIO DE DREW

So, it's like, okay, well, how else could you say that, Andrew? Let unmute yourself. Let's go back and forth real quick.

[20:05] People that pay their bills on time and that are financially responsible.
[20:10] Okay, great. That's that's a great start. Uh to be clear, what does somebody with a 700 plus credit score like? What have they done to get a 700 plus credit score? Because it's not just paying your bills on time. I mean, obviously that's a huge part of it, but
[20:25] long credit history.
[20:30] Um, don't have any collection.
[20:35] Say that again.
[20:40] Long credit history, don't have any collections.
[20:45] How about that?
[20:50] Makes a lot of sense.
[20:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Credit Score Proxy Qualifiers"
❌ "700+ credit score"
✅ "Long credit history"
✅ "Pay bills on time"
✅ "No derogatory marks"
NÚMEROS: 700+ (número proibido)
VISUAL: Lista de qualificadores alternativos, check marks verdes para opções conformes, X vermelho para abordagem direta
[21:00] What do you think the average person's credit score is who has a long credit history, has paid their bills on time, and doesn't have any derogatory marks? What kind of score do you think they're probable to have?
[21:15] 700 plus.
[21:20] So, as an example, if I cannot say if you have a 700 plus credit score, but I can say, hey, if you've got a long credit history, you pay all your bills on time or you don't have any derogatory marks. I got a hell of an opportunity to help you get access to like what are they going to do with that?
[21:40] Like, why why are you looking for somebody with a 700 plus credit score? Is it literally just to get them funding for your program or are you also getting them funding for like inventory or like what are you getting them funding for?
[21:55] It's both.
[22:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Drew's Compliant Hook Strategy"

Funding for inventory ✓

VISUAL: Estrutura de benefícios em lista, ícones de financiamento, timeline de implementação

[22:30] If you want to get a piece of that pie, by the way, new sellers, they have a huge opportunity to get a piece of that pie. And I can show you examples, by the way, of people who are on page 12 that make thousands of dollars every month off their products. Okay?
[22:50] If you have a long credit history, no derogatory marks, and you've continuously paid your bills on time, I can help you get approved for funding for all the inventory you need. So, you barely come out of pocket. Maybe you don't even come out of pocket at all.
[23:10] and I get you started selling on Amazon as fast as I can get I can get you up in as little as two weeks, 30 days. Get everything set up, good to go. [23:45] Wow.

[23:55] you see what I'm saying? Even in your example of where it's literally just you walking on the beach, you could literally say in comparison like the on the qualifications section, you could say, okay, I'll just show you guys this so I'm not just looking at this and you're not.
[24:15] Uh, in this qualifications, you could say if you've had a long credit history, no derogatory marks, and have paid your bills on time. Okay, here's the thing. that whole like want to leave your nineto-five hook. Okay, I think I've talked to you about this before.
[24:35] It is scary as fuck for a person with a job. Like, why are they going to believe you in a textbased ad that you're going to help them quit their 9-to-five job? Like, do you think that breeds trust or uncertainty?
[24:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "9-to-5 Hook Analysis"
❌ "Quit your 9-to-5" = Fear & Skepticism
✅ Alternative: Focus on opportunity, not job quitting
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico
VISUAL: Análise psicológica de reação do público, medidor de confiança vs incerteza, comparação de abordagens
[24:55] Like, look at it like this. Do you think people see that and say, "Damn, dude. This guy, he's the fucking one, man. Like, look at him walking on this beach right here. He's going to help me quit my job." Or do you think they look at it and they say, "Yeah, it's a fucking scam."
[25:15] You see, like that's like in terms of probabilities, more people are probable to look at it and literally be turned off by it than turned on. Does that make sense?
[25:30] So, it's like here's my point, right? Versus if I just say the qualifica like at the top, if I say if you want to get started selling on Amazon for little to nothing out of your own pocket, I can help mentor you and coach you on how to get started.
[25:50] The qualifications are you have a long credit history, you've paid your bills on time, and you have no derogatory marks. You know, DM me and I'll personally get back. Like, I bet that ad would literally crush just as hard.
[26:05] and would also not make you look like a scam by saying the leave the 9 to5 thing. Does that make sense?
[26:15] Dude, makes a 100% sense.
[26:20] Does anybody here disagree with that? See what I'm saying? So, to be clear, it's like, dude, the art of finesse. Okay, the art of finesse. Okay.
CASO: CRYPTO E TRADING AUTOMATIZADO
[26:35] Now, Nick, our uh European traveler here, he put in the chat crypto stuff, automated crypto trading, both that produces passive income. So, that is that is a big tall order right there of just stacked against you non-compliant verbiage. Okay.
[26:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Crypto Compliance Challenge"
❌ Crypto
❌ Automated Trading
❌ Passive Income
Triple threat of non-compliance
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico
VISUAL: Lista de palavras problemáticas, ícones de alerta, nível de dificuldade de conformidade destacado
[27:00] So, to be clear, we have to be able to sell that opportunity and also make it known that it's crypto without saying that it's crypto. So as an example, we have the art of synonym usage.
[27:15] So as I mentioned when I talked about passive income, I said passive returns. That's compliant in the eyes of Facebook. So instead of saying income, the synonym to that is return. You get what I'm saying?
[27:30] And and the human being reading that still knows I'm talking about financial financial uh situations. You see what I'm saying? It's not like I'm talking about some random stuff. um to be clear.
[27:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Crypto Synonym Strategy"
❌ Crypto → ✅ Coins, Digital Assets
❌ Passive Income → ✅ Passive Returns
Visual pairing with Coinbase-style apps
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico
VISUAL: Mapa de sinônimos, setas de substituição, mockup de app estilo Coinbase sem usar marca
[27:50] So I also need a different word for crypto because crypto can also get flagged a lot. Let's think of some synonyms for crypto. I would think of coins. And in addition to that, I could also through the visuals that I pair in the ad emphasize that by maybe showing some type of app that looks like Coinbase.
[28:10] Have you ever seen one of those shirts that says Cornhub and it looks just like the Pornhub logo?
[28:20] Yep.
[28:25] Little Facebook. Obviously, it doesn't say Pornhub on it. It's like I've seen people run ads wearing shirts that just like use a word like that in the Pornhub style logo and it gets a lot of attention. Definitely gets a good thumb stop.
[28:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Visual Implication Strategy"
Cornhub parody example

Visual recognition without violation
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico
VISUAL: Exemplo de paródia de logo, técnica de reconhecimento visual, ícone de thumb stop
[28:45] So, my point being that's an example of that. I have to be able to visually like pair what I'm talking about without actually saying it. So, as an example, Nick, it's like you could just say because we we have uh there's there's I think Robin in the group I know does this with uh automated trading bot stuff and I think there's if I'm not mistaken like two or three other of you guys that do that, too.

[29:30] a lot of these coins and you know opportunities that exist in some of these new most recently created financial markets and you know here's an example and you might like show an app that like looks like Coinbase but it's not actually Coinbase so it doesn't get flagged as an example just like the same color blue you know and like a word in the middle that looks like the Coinbase logo with the same font but maybe it's your logo as an example instead or like the word of your company or whatever it is.

[31:55] However, I'm not going to say that out loud because it's non-compliant. Okay, implied claims through environment. Okay.

Synonyms
Implied Environmental Claims
Filler Word Spacing

Okay, that's really it.
[32:35] The art of taking once again non-compliant ads and making them compliant really just comes down to those variables to to master it.

IMPORTÂNCIA DOS CURSOS DE CONFORMIDADE

[34:30] some instances if I'm getting a lot of disapprovals and I'm confused on why, I'll go take it literally on the spot. Okay?
[34:40] So, I just want to be clear. All of you should go out there if you're advertising something that is borderline non-compliant or if you've seen any rejections or if you're as oblivious as Drew was, no offense, please go and take the compliance courses from these platforms so you're aware of them and not creating liabilities for yourself.
[35:05] you're you're essentially lucky that Facebook, believe it or not, denies your ads and doesn't allow it to run because if somebody reported you to a government regulatory body in that example, it'd be a lot worse than Facebook disapproving you in that example. Does that make sense?
[35:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Facebook Rejections = Protection"
Ad disapproval is better than legal action
Regulatory body reports are worse
Platform protecting you from yourself
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico
VISUAL: Comparação de consequências, escala de gravidade (rejeição de ad vs ação legal), ícone de proteção
CASO: BEFORE/AFTER MÉDICO
[35:30] Um, yeah, Jeremy, there's an interesting one with regard to medical. I know we've got some medical people on the call. So what I noticed is some of my other agencies in the dental space and medical space, they get away with running video ads that include before and after images whenever we run them, even if we try and hide them within the body of the ad, they get rejected.
[36:00] So we have two theories. One is theirs get rejected after a certain time as well. We just haven't seen them before they got rejected. Or two is they know something that we don't.
[36:15] It's probable that they're at a low level of spend and that they get through and because you're in the niche that you see those ads as a result of that and then they get denied.
[36:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Medical Before/After Strategy"
Low spend = lower scrutiny initially
Filler technique applies to medical too
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico
VISUAL: Teoria de aprovação baseada em volume de gasto, curva de detecção ao longo do tempo
[36:35] Here's the same logic as what I just articulated applies to what you said. So, I have a guy, he does this, he does this really well. I think I actually talked to you about him on one of our one-on-one calls. He's the guy that does those like composite veneers, the Dr. Jordan guy or whatever his name was.
[36:55] Oh, yeah. Yeah.
[37:00] And I don't remember the guy. Yeah, but yeah. So, what what he'll do is he'll start by just articulating what the person's smile looked like and showing visuals of it. And then it'll cut to him talking about what the process was and like what specific things about the smile they might wanted to have changed and then they show the after right after that.
[37:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Dr. Jordan Veneer Strategy"
Before images with narration
10 second dentist explanation (filler)
After images
Macro shots of process
NÚMEROS: 10 seconds (filler duration)
VISUAL: Estrutura de vídeo em 3 atos, timeline mostrando separação de before/after, close-ups macro destacados
[37:30] So it's like before images, videos, like you know up close like fucking macro shots of the thing, cut to the dentist sitting there talking about it for maybe like 10 seconds and then they cut to the after right after that and that finesses around that system as an example.
[37:50] So to be clear, it's the same logic as what I just implied with uh make x amount of dollars in x time frame. You have to space it by still implying that that's the case but putting fillers in between so it's separated. You're just tricking the automated system in that example. Does that make sense?
[38:10] Gotcha. Yeah. So, put them like with a fill in between. Understood.
TREINAMENTO DE IA E DETECÇÃO
[38:20] Yeah. You know how look look at it like this, right? So, like everybody's become a lot more educated on AI in the most recent year, I'd say. And one of the most uh I wouldn't say common things that people are learning, but regardless, I mean, you learn about it if you're paying attention to AI at all, is how the models are trained. Okay?
[38:45] So, the models are trained just by putting a fuckload of data into it. Like as an example, in the medical space, there is an AI that's dedicated to identifying breast cancer in the earliest stages. Well, how the fuck does it know that?
[39:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "AI Training & Detection Logic"
AI trained on massive datasets
Breast cancer detection example
Tens to hundreds of thousands of images
NÚMEROS: Tens of thousands to hundreds of thousands of training images
VISUAL: Diagrama de treinamento de IA, dataset visual, processo de reconhecimento de padrões
[39:10] They train it on photos that have historically been medically cataloged of breast cancer in the earliest stages and in the later stages. And they import, let's say, tens of thousands to hundreds of thousands of images into this thing. So when it sees another new image, it can identify based on all the historical data what that looks like.
[39:35] So, as an example, if you were trying to train an AI, if you were Facebook in this example, you're trying to train your AI on how to identify before and afters, what would you do? Well, you would take all of what looks like before and afters.
[39:55] And think about the most common ones, like photos side by side, photos like this, you know, stacked on top of each other. Um, the ones that you would have a harder time identifying would be the ones like what I just said where there's a filler in between and they're not literally side by side. Does that make sense?
[40:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Facebook AI Training Patterns"
Side-by-side images = easy detection
Stacked images = easy detection
Separated with filler = harder detection
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico
VISUAL: Exemplos de layouts de before/after, padrões fáceis vs difíceis de detectar, esquema de posicionamento
[40:20] Yeah. And would you put the before or the after first?
[40:25] Well, you would not matter. You can technically do whatever you want. I'm just saying you just need a filler in between one and the other. Does that make sense?
[40:35] Got it. Got it. Got it. But just just from a marketing perspective, would you agree that it's better to put the after first to show the outcome before you show the
[40:45] No. To be fair, like I don't know why, but I just thought of uh the cake Tik Tockers where they'll show like a fully completed like crazy looking cake
[40:55] and then they'll the process of how to build the cake after, you know?
[41:00] Got it. Got it. Got it. I guess you could start it with the after and it's still make a lot of s honestly probably more sense to start it with the after and then then breakess from there.
[41:15] I agree. Yeah. No, thank you. That is very useful.
[41:20] Of course.
VÍDEO VS IMAGEM COM TEXTO
[41:25] Okay, cool. Anybody else have questions, contributions, things you guys have finessed?
[41:30] I do have a question.
[41:35] It seems like uh and maybe this is specifically for retargeting ads. It seems like there's been a wave of people that are just like put text on a still image and use that for retargeting. But if you're trying to be to finesse, it's harder to do with limited amount of of space for text.
[41:55] Do you think video ads with actual voice doing all this makes a lot more sense and are more effective than just those text ads like the one that that Drew had?
[42:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Video vs Text-on-Image Ads"
Text-on-image = limited finesse space
Video = more wiggle room for compliance
Both can work if compliant
NÚMEROS: Nenhum número específico
VISUAL: Comparação de formatos, espaço disponível para finesse destacado, análise de flexibilidade
[42:15] Yeah, I mean to be fair, like you know, Drew's ad is effective. It's just non-compliant. So he can still accomplish what he's attempting to accomplish there with different words. you just you you don't have as much wiggle room, I guess would be the right word to use, on your ability to finesse versus in videos, I I just have more wiggle room and I have more uh leeway to finesse, I guess, would be the right way to articulate it, you know?
[42:45] So, if if you can if you can do it successfully with an image and text, then I mean, sure, test it out either way, but I wouldn't necessarily be biased one way or the other. I mean, we just always try both, you know? We don't want to be right. We just want to make money.
[43:05] Makes sense.
ENCERRAMENTO
[43:10] All right, cool. Any other questions, comments, contributions?
[43:20] All right, sweet. Looking forward to seeing everybody at the upcoming Q4 mastermind October 19th and 20th. You guys have questions or anything you need to know in between now and then, hit me up, drop it in the group chat. I'll upload the recording once it's done. And uh yeah, go out there and make some money. Talk soon.
[43:45] Thanks, Jody.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
700+ credit score - Score específico que causou violação legal
$80,000/month - Aluguel da mansão de Tai Lopez em Beverly Hills
4 carros exóticos - Aventador, 458/488, Gallardo, Maserati usados como contexto visual
$10,000-$100,000/month - Exemplo de claim não-conforme em 60 dias
"Thousands to tens of thousands" - Exemplo de claim conforme (vago)
10 seconds - Duração ideal de filler entre before/after em ads médicos
2-4 weeks - Timeline de setup conforme para negócio Amazon
Page 12 - Exemplo de vendedores Amazon fazendo milhares/mês
1 vez por ano mínimo - Frequência recomendada para fazer cursos de conformidade
Tens to hundreds of thousands - Imagens usadas para treinar IA de detecção
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
1. Técnica de Sinônimos:
❌ Passive Income → ✅ Passive Returns
❌ Crypto → ✅ Coins, Digital Assets, Alternative Investments
❌ Wealth → ✅ Long-term Financial Growth (contexto dependente)
❌ 700+ Credit Score → ✅ Long credit history + Pay bills on time + No derogatory marks
2. Claims Ambientais Implícitos (Método Tai Lopez):
Usar ambiente luxuoso (mansão, carros exóticos) para comunicar riqueza
NUNCA verbalizar claims diretos de riqueza
Deixar o ambiente fazer o trabalho de vendas
Manter script 100% conforme enquanto visual comunica não-conformidade
3. Técnica de Espaçamento com Filler Words:
❌ "$10K/month in 60 days"
✅ "Thousands...some even tens of thousands...paid monthly...couple months to start"
Separar valor monetário e timeframe com palavras de preenchimento
Trucar sistemas automatizados de detecção de IA
4. Contexto Profissional vs Amador:
Amador: Balenciaga, praia, carros, "quit your 9-5"
Profissional: Terno, escritório, certificações, linguagem educacional
Mesmas palavras podem ser conformes ou não baseado em contexto
5. Before/After Médico:
Mostrar before com narração
10 segundos de dentista explicando processo (FILLER)
Mostrar after
Usar macro shots e cortes para separar
NUNCA lado a lado ou empilhado
6. Visual Pairing sem Violação:
Exemplo: Coinbase-style app sem usar marca Coinbase
Mesmas cores, fonte similar, logo próprio
Reconhecimento visual sem menção direta
"Cornhub" parody strategy
ESTRUTURA DE CONFORMIDADE:
4 Pilares Fundamentais:
CONTEXTO & AMBIENTE
Como e onde você comunica importa mais que O QUE você diz
Ambiente profissional permite mais liberdade verbal
Ambiente casual/luxuoso requer mais cuidado
SINÔNIMOS ESTRATÉGICOS
Substituir palavras proibidas por sinônimos conformes
Manter significado claro para humanos
Passar por detecção automatizada
CLAIMS AMBIENTAIS IMPLÍCITOS
Comunicar através de visual, não verbal
Deixar audiência fazer a conexão
Nunca verbalizar o que o ambiente já comunica
ESPAÇAMENTO COM FILLERS
Separar elementos proibidos quando juntos
Usar palavras de preenchimento entre claims
Trucar treinamento de IA de detecção
Leis e Regulações Mencionadas:
Fair Credit Act - Proíbe discriminação baseada em credit score
Misdemeanor Financial Crime - Violação pode resultar em processo criminal
EU Compliance Standards - Cada país tem suas próprias regulações
Germany - Alguns dos padrões mais rigorosos do mundo
Por Que Plataformas Se Importam:
Evitar multas governamentais
Evitar processo criminal
Proteger a plataforma, não necessariamente o anunciante
Facebook rejeitar ad é MELHOR que ação legal posterior
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
$80K/month - Custo de rental de Beverly Hills (Tai Lopez)
$10K-$100K/month - Range de income claim não-conforme típico
Thousands to tens of thousands - Range conforme (intencionalmente vago)
Trillion dollar company - Referência conforme a Amazon
"Little to nothing out of pocket" - Claim conforme de investimento baixo
EXEMPLOS PRÁTICOS REFORMULADOS:
Drew's Amazon Business (ANTES - NÃO-CONFORME):
Walking on beach
"700+ credit score required"
"Want to quit your 9-5"
Visual: casual, laptop lifestyle
Drew's Amazon Business (DEPOIS - CONFORME):
Professional setting or removed beach context
"Long credit history, pay bills on time, no derogatory marks"
Focus on opportunity, not job quitting
"Little to nothing out of pocket"
"2-4 weeks to get started"
"Page 12 sellers making thousands/month"
Nick's Crypto Trading (CONFORME):
"Alternative investments"
"Digital assets" ou "coins"
"New financial markets"
"Automated trading bot"
"Passive returns" (não passive income)
Visual: Coinbase-style app sem marca Coinbase
"Blue chip" focus
Proof of "consistent monthly returns" (vago)
EDUCAÇÃO CONTÍNUA:
Meta Blueprint - Curso oficial de conformidade do Facebook
Frequência: Mínimo 1x por ano
On-demand: Quando receber múltiplas rejeições
Todas as plataformas oferecem cursos gratuitos
Compliance muda constantemente - educação contínua essencial
DETECÇÃO DE IA - COMO FUNCIONA:
Treinada em milhares/centenas de milhares de exemplos
Identifica padrões mais comuns (lado a lado, empilhado)
Dificuldade em detectar padrões separados com fillers
Before/after médico: fácil detectar se junto, difícil se separado
MENTALIDADE CHAVE:
"We don't want to be right. We just want to make money."
Testar ambos formatos (vídeo e imagem)
Não ser tendencioso - deixar dados decidirem
Facebook rejeitar é PROTEÇÃO, não punição
Ação legal é muito pior que rejeição de ad
DURAÇÃO: ~43:45 minutos
FORMATO: Group coaching call / Masterclass sobre conformidade em ads
EVENTO MENCIONADO: Q4 Mastermind - October 19-20
INSTRUTOR: Jeremy (Jody)
CASO PRINCIPAL: Drew's Amazon FBA ad violation
